lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY guarded, light (NOT yet runtime-proven). Closes both devopsservice writes: POST /v1/devops-services (createdevopsservice) and DELETE /v1/devops-services/{devops_service_id} (deletedevopsservice). A devops-service is a control-plane CI/CD workspace (no obviously-billable infra), so this attempts a guarded REAL create -> capture id -> delete; but because the body is UNPROVEN (no data/api_bodies.json entry; docs JS-rendered) it is coverage-only: the create is optional+broad expect_status so a 400/422 (missing/wrong field) still records coverage without failing the run. devops_service_id is captured from a 2xx create when possible, else soft-captured from the read-only list so the DELETE and id-bound GETs still target a real id. The check-duplication GET pre-checks the chosen name. probe-reads exercises showdevopsservice + checkdeletabledevopsservice. Bodies (name/description) best-effort from field semantics; UNPROVEN.

steps.name: list-devops-services
steps._note: Soft-capture an existing id so the DELETE / id-bound GETs target a real resource even if the guarded create below 4xx-es.

steps.name: create-devops-service
steps._note: Guarded create. Body (name/description) best-effort, UNPROVEN. Capture path $.devops_service.id is a guess; soft so a different envelope still records coverage (the list-captured id covers DELETE). Expected 400/422 if a required field is missing or 409 if the name exists.

steps.name: delete-devops-service
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Deletes the captured/soft-captured devops-service. optional+broad so a 4xx records coverage; on a shared account this may target a soft-captured pre-existing id, so it is never required to succeed (expected 403/404).
